LAW AND MOTION 302,PLAINTIFF JULIE LEE'S NOTICE OF MOTION TO SET ASIDE VOID JUDGMENT.PLAINTIFF JULIE LEE'S "MOTION TO SET ASIDE VOID JUDGMENT" IS DENIED. LEE'S MOTION IS PREMISED ON NOTIONS THAT SHE TIMELY FILED AND PERSONALLY SERVED A PROPER STATEMENT OF DISQUALIFICATION ON TRIAL JUDGE NEWTON LAM BEFORE HE ENTERED JUDGMENT. (CCP 170.1, 170.3.) HOWEVER, JUDGE LAM STRUCK LEE'S DISQUALIFICATION STATEMENT IN AN ORDER ACCURATELY FINDING THAT LEE'S STATEMENT WAS NEITHER TIMELY FILED NOR PERSONALLY SERVED AND STATED NO LEGAL GROUNDS FOR DISQUALIFICATION. (CCP 170.4(B).) COUNSEL FOR DEFENDANT IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE COURT'S RULING IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER JR., CLERK: M. GOODMAN, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR#8600, MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM, REPORTED. =(302/RBU)

Notice Of Motion To Set Aside Void Judgment
Off Calendar Feb-28-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Mandarin Court Interpreter Carmen Li is present.

No appearances by the Parties. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF JULIE LEE'S Notice Of Motion To Set Aside Void Judgment.

Plaintiff Julie Lee's "motion to set aside void judgment" is denied. Lee's motion is premised on notions that she timely filed and personally served a proper statement of disqualification on trial judge Newton Lam before he entered judgment. (CCP 170.1, 170.3.) However, Judge Lam struck Lee's disqualification statement in an order accurately finding that Lee's statement was neither timely filed nor personally served and stated no legal grounds for disqualification. (CCP 170.4(b).)

Counsel for defendant is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/RBU)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Summons
Off Calendar Apr-23-2018 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances.

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANT EDDIE WANG's Motion To Quash Summons is DENIED. Plaintiff Julie Lee has shown that she has effected and now completed valid substituted service on Mr. Wang by the mailing that occurred on April 12, 2018. Mr. Wang must file a response to the first amended complaint no later than May 22, 2018. Counsel is admonished that motions like this one rarely achieve any purpose other than increasing costs for everyone. The prevailing party shall submit a [proposed] written order that tracks the Court's ruling, verbatim.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, e-mail: torreanom@aol.org (L&M302)
